user_query:
Please read the ETL plan and related references under input/ and record every step into our local ETL activity logs. Specifically:

1) Parse input/etl_plan.md for the listed operations and log them as distinct entries by their category (ingest, transform, validate, schema, aggregate, query, filter, export, sample, profile, pipeline, visualize). Use the exact phrases from the files when logging values so the audit trail matches the plan.
2) Parse input/schema.yaml and record the schema entry mentioned there (including the version tag) into the schema activity log.
3) Parse input/data_quality_rules.csv and record three separate validation entries reflecting those rules and their outcomes.
4) After recording all entries, export the full activity history to JSON and CSV and place copies at output/etl_export.json and output/etl_export.csv.
5) Create an audit report at output/audit.md that includes: a title section "# ETL Audit", a "Counts by Command" section with a per-category count, a "Logged Entries" section citing at least three concrete examples from the export (by type and value text), and sections "Notes & Assumptions" and "Next Steps".
6) Create output/commands_run.jsonl capturing each command you executed to record the entries. Each line should be a JSON object with keys: type and value, reflecting the category and the exact text logged. Include one line per logged entry.

Constraints:
- Use only relative workspace paths in your logs and reports (e.g., input/…, output/…). Do not include absolute paths.
- Ensure the export files and the audit report reflect the specific strings from the plan (e.g., the dataset filename, job name, pipeline name, schema version, sampling seed, and email domain exclusions).
- Write all deliverables under output/ as specified.
